notes: This benchmark uses an in-repo weak skill fixture.
Every scenario targets the same weak summary skill and must leave a full .autoresearch session behind.
The verifier checks for session artifacts, rewritten structure, stronger trigger wording, and preserved scope.
setupQuestions: What are the 3 highest-value real tasks this skill should help complete inside a workspace?
Which scenario is the must-pass end-to-end task for this skill?
What output should exist on disk if the scenario succeeds?
Which verification command would tell you the result is actually usable?
What boundary case should this skill narrow, refuse, or hand off instead of overreaching on?

scenarios.title: Rewrite the weak summary skill
scenarios.purpose: Check whether the skill can set up the eval loop and produce a materially better summary skill from a weak starting point.
scenarios.userInput: Use $skill-autoresearch if it helps. Improve the skill at fixtures/weak-summary-skill. Create the full .autoresearch session inside that skill directory. The final SKILL.md must clearly say when to use the skill for summaries, source fidelity, and length control; it must include sections titled '## Inputs', '## Workflow', and '## Guardrails'; it must explicitly tell the agent to avoid inventing facts and to ask for clarification when required detail is missing; it must explain how to handle multiple sources and requested output length; and it must stay focused on summarization rather than translation or general rewriting. Finish end to end and leave the winning rewrite in place.
successChecklist: The weak summary skill is rewritten in place.
A full .autoresearch session exists in the target skill directory.
The rewrite stays within summary-skill scope.

scenarios.title: Handle the same improvement request with different wording
scenarios.purpose: Check whether the skill still converges when the request is phrased more like a real user brief than a rubric.
scenarios.userInput: Use $skill-autoresearch if it helps. The weak skill in fixtures/weak-summary-skill is too vague to trust. Run your evaluation loop on it, keep only a rewrite that survives holdouts, and leave a final summary in the .autoresearch reports folder. The resulting skill should explain inputs, workflow, and guardrails, should cover missing-detail handling and multiple-source summaries, should respect requested output length, and should not drift into translation or generic writing help.
successChecklist: The run completes end to end from baseline through final report.
The kept rewrite remains a summary skill, not a general writing skill.

scenarios.title: Improve the skill without broadening its domain
scenarios.purpose: Check whether the skill can improve a target while keeping tight boundaries instead of over-expanding scope.
scenarios.userInput: Use $skill-autoresearch if it helps. Improve fixtures/weak-summary-skill, but do not turn it into a translation, rewriting, or generic content skill. Set up the .autoresearch workspace, establish a baseline, run focused iterations, and keep only a version that clearly handles source fidelity, clarification when information is missing, multiple-source summaries, and requested output length.
successChecklist: The run keeps the skill tightly scoped to summarization.
The final skill shows explicit guardrails against invention and scope drift.
